content.title: The Safar moon has been sighted in Bangladesh
content.subtitle: At the beginning of a new month, learn an important Sunnah.
content.body:
Alhamdulillah! The moon marking the beginning of the sacred month of Safar 1448 AH has been sighted in the skies of Bangladesh. Upon sighting the new moon, the Prophet ﷺ would recite:

اَللهُ أَكْبَرُ اَللَّهُمَّ أَهِلَّهُ عَلَيْنَا بِالْأَمْنِ وَالْإِيْمَانِ وَالسَّلاَمَةِ وَالْإِسْلاَمِ وَالتَّوْفِيْقِ لِمَا تُحِبُّ وَتَرْضَى، رَبِّىْ وَرَبُّكَ اللهُ

Translation:

Allah is the Greatest. O Allah, let this moon appear over us with security and faith, safety and Islam, and with the ability to do what You love and are pleased with. (O moon!) My Lord and your Lord is Allah.

References:

Sunan al-Darimi (1687–1688), Jami' al-Tirmidhi (3451), Mishkat al-Masabih (2428), Al-Silsilah al-Sahihah (1816).

Fasting on the Ayyam al-Bid of Safar

The Ayyam al-Bid fasts for the month of Safar should be observed on 28, 29, and 30 July, corresponding to Tuesday, Wednesday, and Thursday.

Learn more about the virtues of Ayyam al-Bid fasting here:

https://muslimsday.com/blog/আইয়ামে-বীজ-রোজার-ফজিলত

Akheri Chahar Shomba: An Unfounded Practice

In our country, a bid'ah (religious innovation) associated with the month of Safar is widely observed, almost as a national custom. This is the practice known as Akheri Chahar Shomba.

However, the commonly circulated events and acts of worship associated with this day are not supported by any authentic hadith. Therefore, let us protect ourselves and our families from such innovations and cultural practices falsely attributed to Islam.

To learn more, visit the Muslims Day blog:

https://muslimsday.com/blog/আখেরী-চাহার-সোম্বা-বিদআত

Important Note:

If the Hijri date is not updating in the app, please make sure your phone is connected to the internet and pull down to refresh the app's home page, just as you would on Facebook or YouTube. In shaa Allah, the issue will be resolved.

footer_button.hint: You power, we spread more Dawah
